terms.definition: The field of building systems that perceive, reason, learn, and act to achieve goals that traditionally required human intelligence.
AI systems designed for specific tasks (classification, routing, game play, generation) rather than general human-level intelligence across domains.
A hypothetical class of systems with flexible, human-level intelligence across many tasks and environments; distinct from today's narrow AI products.
AI approaches that represent knowledge explicitly (rules, logic, graphs) and reason over it, often without learning from large datasets.
An entity that perceives its environment through sensors and acts through actuators to maximize a goal or utility; a unifying lens for many AI systems.
